entry.title: Re: Usecase of Flight order creation for a customer reser...
entry.content: Thanks Jignesh this is something we already reviewed. We wanted to have real time experience use case to confirm how the billing and invoicing will flow form flight order.

entry.title: Re: How to use a API publish on integration suite in SAP...
entry.content:
Hi,

 

did you maintain a connection in the DMC_Cloud Webserver to you new Webserver so the Webservice can be called from Cloud (more information https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-digital-manufacturing/production-connectivity-model/setting-up-connections-between-web-servers )

 

Thanks,

Marcel

entry.title: Re: Compass - Simulation - Currency conversion not detect...
entry.content: Hi Chen, Thanks for your fast response! I have added the screenshots now. Please reach it out if you need more information! Kind regards, Eric

entry.title: Decentralized EWM on an SAP S/4HANA stack
entry.content:
Dears,

I would like to understand, what would be the possible impact of changing below configuration on already running S/4HANA on-premise 2021?

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> SCM Extended Warehouse Management -> Extended Warehouse Management -> Enable Decentralized EWM

It is important to clarify, that we ARE NOT using EWM (neither Emended nor Decentralized), but for some reason, during postings in MIGO transaction, system is "looking for" EWM tables to update (checked in de-bug mode). During this time of approximate 30 mins, documents posted are locked in SM12. We found out, IF Decentralized EWM is activated in SPRO, the system skip searching and directly post without DB block.

Help information says:

Note:

You must not change the configuration of whether EWM is embedded or decentralized once transaction data exists, because this would disrupt existing processes and documents.

For more information about EWM deployment options, see SAP Note 1606493.

So, we have transactional data, BUT it is not in EWM tables (as we didn't activate EWM), hence question is: In case, we change set up to "Decentralized EWM", when we actually don't have such, whether to expect any negative impact on system?

Thank you!

entry.title: Datasphere: Table Function - Union with Tuple instead of a Table
entry.content:
Hello, 

I'm creating a SQL-based View in Datasphere. I have a requirement to create multiple versions from the same record I'm looping over. I have inititalized a table variable to hold my result and have created a cursor over my source table. Based on each iteration, I manually create the tuple that I would like to insert as my new record. The issue that I'm facing is appending to my result table. I want to overwrite my result table as the union of itself with the new record, but the syntax check fails as a "from" clause is expected implying that I have to use a table. I know that what I'm trying to achieve is possible on other DBs and SQL flavors but cannot seem to find the equivalent on HANA. Any ideas? I have attached a screenshot of the code snippet.

Thanks!

entry.title: UI5 Fragment Changes Not Reflecting Even After Cache Clear & Rebuild
entry.content: When I create NotificationType.fragment.xml and its corresponding NotificationType.js, everything works fine. However, when I try to modify the fragment—such as adding a Combo Box or changing a text—none of the changes appear. It seems like the fragment is read only once, and after that, any modifications are not detected. Even if I clear the cache and rebuild the project, the issue persists.

entry.title: Re: From User Exits to RAP: Adopting a Clean Core Approach in S/4HANA
entry.content: Excellent blog and this is really helpful for clean core projects!

entry.title: I am creating material its showing me assigned number ranges is the last available number. M3322
entry.content:
Hello

I am creating material MM01

Its showing me assigned number 0000000000000004 is the last available number.

Message No. M3322

entry.title: Re: I am creating material its showing me assigned number...
entry.content:
Hello @NIK1707 

The long text of the message M3322 explains the situation clearly:

You need to verify material number ranges settings.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Datasphere Internal error: SQL View - Resource governor for 'cursors' exceeded
entry.content: @Vitaliy-R thanks for your input. I tried reducing the number of temporary tables where possible.However, ultimately, all the data comes from 2 ADSOs on BW Bridge. There are 2 layers of views underlying the SQL view I'm working on and the dataset I'm working with is only ~1k records.

entry.title: The Broad Spectrum of Supply Chain Security
entry.content:
By Jay Thoden van Velzen, Technical Advisor, Office of the CSO

This blog discusses topics also covered in The Future of Supply Chain podcast, Episode 83: Supply Chain Security, Scope and Definitions. If you prefer podcasts over blogs, click the link!

 

Supply Chain Security From Different Viewpoints

There is increasing understanding that supply chain security is important to the resilience of business operations. However, since it covers many different aspects, we often misunderstand each other. The problem is that supply chain security means very different things depending on your functional area and from which viewpoint you look at it. Supply chain security covers a broad spectrum, and the term is used for widely different challenges. This leads to confusion, and people using metrics from one area to make an argument in another. It is not that simple.

To an operational business executive keeping a product line going, supply chain security refers to the physical movement of goods and components. To enterprise IT leaders, it is who and how contractors access company systems and facilities, and what technology providers they bring in. To software developers, supply chain security refers to their software dependency chain. When they discuss their supply chain challenges, they will have trouble understanding each other.

In all cases, we’re dealing with suppliers. But the reduction of risk in each area requires a very different approach. SAP, as a provider of business cloud solutions, touches on the entire spectrum of supply chain security. Hopefully, our broad experience can help get our three leaders on the same page.

 

Surveying the Spectrum

In broad outlines, we can distinguish between three main categories:

  • Physical Supply Chain Security
  • Digital Supply Chain Security
  • Software Supply Chain Security

The cases mentioned above fit rather neatly into those categories. However, there is also significant overlap between these categories, and it is therefore better to refer to supply chain security as a continuing spectrum. Just how in a color spectrum we can still distinguish between red, yellow or blue even though the scale is continuous, so are these three markers still useful to help us navigate the field.

Physical Supply Chain Security

At the outer edge of the spectrum, we see the risks to the supply chain with direct physical, material impact. Resilience must be built into the supply chain to withstand the impact of conflict, natural disasters, or sanctions. In recent years, we have seen pandemic restrictions on different regions at different times have significant cascading impact on supply chains. Conflicts have made major transport routes less safe or cut them off. Sanctions have forced re-alignment of energy supply lines and geopolitical tensions have forced relocation of semiconductor manufacturing and restricted technology transfer- and export regimes.

Another example in the physical realm is product tampering. How are products – whether supplies or finished products –protected from being tampered with and protected from theft in storage depots and during transit? Even if you have taken appropriate measures in your own facilities, inevitably there are spots in your supply chain you don’t have direct control over.

Aside from tampering, a similar problem comes up with component integrity. Not all suppliers operate with the same integrity, and may offer common electronic components, for instance, that are not up to standard or code. Using such components in consumer electronics can have health and safety consequences through malfunction.

Digital Supply Chain Security

Component integrity is also a digital problem. Such components are listed on a digital marketplace, with seemingly correct standard dimensions and specification, offered by a company that changes its name every 6 months. Verifying that the marketplace performs due diligence on the listed suppliers and their product offerings falls under digital supply chain security.

A key digital supply chain security challenge is the provisioning and monitoring of access to your organization’s systems, networks and facilities by third party contractors and service providers. These can be employees of a system integrator that help manage IT systems but also includes third party facility maintenance or cleaning staff. Such contractors are often issued access badges and credentials to internal systems to conduct their work. Others are treated as visitors and may need to be always accompanied by an employee host.

For managed service providers (MSP) and Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) scenarios, the dynamic reverses. Instead of provisioning access to third-party staff to systems you control, now your organization uses systems operated by a third-party supplier. This could be a transition from self-hosted e-mail servers operated by contractors to a cloud-based e-mail service, or from on-premise SAP ERP systems to SAP cloud ERP services.

Software Supply Chain Security

The final category covers the software that your organization chooses to use or include into your products and services. This runs from SaaS through Platform- and Infrastructure-as-a-Service (PaaS, IaaS), to commercial software solutions deployed in your IT landscape. This requires verification that such suppliers meet your security and compliance requirements, have effective security programs in place, and fix vulnerabilities and misconfigurations within an acceptable time frame.

It also includes any open source software (OSS) solutions that you use in your organization, or libraries you include in your own development projects. OSS components often help reduce cost and accelerate development, but they come with their own risks. Only the largest OSS projects can match the level of support commercial providers offer for their solutions. Strictly speaking, there is no supplier in OSS, unless you select an enterprise license or service contract. Many OSS components themselves have dependencies on other OSS projects. Managing OSS dependency chains and keeping components up to date yourself require significant effort that is often underestimated.

Software supply chain concerns have raised to the level of governmental policy and regulation.

 

Mitigating Supply Chain Security Risks

Leaving aside any physical security measures, we can reduce supply chain security risks with these mitigation approaches:

  • Business Planning and Supply Chain Resilience
  • Third Party Risk Management (TPRM) and Compliance
  • IAM and Zero Trust
  • Vulnerability and Patch Management

As shown in the diagram below, they apply differently across the risk spectrum.

Business Planning and Supply Chain Resilience

Business Planning and Supply Chain Resilience – and planning for supply chain resilience – is critical to manage an ever-shifting portfolio of suppliers of goods and services across different jurisdictions, customs controls, and tax regimes. Security risks to the supply chain must be included alongside other business risks in business planning, while resilience must be baked into the supply chain to have the agility to adjust when circumstances beyond your control require you to.

SAP has solutions in its portfolio, such as SAP Integrated Business Planning for Supply Chain and other SAP Supply Chain Management Software, SAP Business Network, or SAP International Trade Management, that can help with this.

The bar for this in the diagram extends to everywhere in the supply chain where we have suppliers, as well as the impact of geopolitics.

Third Party Risk Management and Compliance

TPRM and compliance verification are necessary to ensure that your goods and service providers conduct business in a way that meets your security and compliance requirements, as well as risk appetite. This includes the verification of audit reports from suppliers, but also the maturity of their security programs. It covers the integrity and reliability of the supplier and its leaders, adherence to ethical business practices, and the supplier’s likelihood to deliver on its commitments. Such assessments extend to the supplier’s suppliers, as well. Ultimately, we must decide whether any supplier is one you’re willing to do business with.

The bar for this in the diagram extends to everywhere in the supply chain where we have suppliers.

IAM and Zero Trust

IAM and Zero Trust governs identity and access to internal systems as well as managed services and cloud applications, both for employees and contractors. Zero Trust involves features such as Multi Factor Authentication (MFA), device identity, and network-, attribute- and policy-based access controls. The topic goes far beyond the scope of this blog, but the NIST Special Publication 800-207 on Zero Trust Architecture is a readable introduction and guide.

The bar in the diagram for this extends further to the right than in the previous two, despite the lack of suppliers in open source. This is to cover OSS applications or solutions you have built yourself from OSS but still need included in your authentication and authorization scope. It starts where you provide access to third parties to your systems and facilities.

Vulnerability and Patch Management

For whatever software you use, whether managed by a service provider or operated yourself, you have a responsibility for vulnerability and patch management – be it with increasing responsibilities the further right in the spectrum we go. For MSPs and SaaS solutions, you have an obligation to verify that your suppliers are meeting their commitments. For commercial and open source software you operate yourself, you are fully responsible for keeping the software up to date when patches become available, and that the software is configured securely.

Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) is often associated with software supply chain management, but I think it is better associated with Third Party Risk Management and Compliance. After all, it is intended to give customers a means to conduct their risk assessment in using the software or service of that supplier.  

For the provider of the software or service, the key strategy is Vulnerability and Patch Management. If those processes work well, the SBOM – including its software dependencies – will be of better quality and therefore make a stronger impression on your customers in their TPRM evaluation.

 

Cross-Functional Effort

From these approaches, it is clear that these will be covered by different functional areas in the organization. Business planning falls under business operations. TPRM and compliance under Governance, Risk and Compliance (GRC), while IAM and Zero Trust and vulnerability management cover IT, cybersecurity and development teams. Covering supply chain security risk is therefore a cross-functional effort.

None of these approaches are easy. But there are suppliers, including SAP, that can support you in that journey.

 

More Information

More information can be found through the following links:

  • The Future of Supply Chain podcast, Episode 83: Supply Chain Security, Scope and Definitions
  • Government’s Role in Increasing Software Supply Chain Security: A Toolbox for Policy Makers (interface, March 2023)
  • SAP Supply Chain Management Software
  • SAP Business Network
  • SAP International Trade Management
  • NIST Special Publication 800-207 on Zero Trust Architecture

entry.title: Re: Reforma Tributária LC 214 - Nota Fiscal Writer J1B1N/...
entry.content: Olá, eu notei, é como se eles fossem um IPI, fica de fora no total dos produtos, mas aparecem no total da NF, como podemos questionar a SAP sobre?

entry.title: Re: Planned Independent Requirement not consumed by withd...
entry.content:
Looks like this is normal.

OSS note 812592 - MS64 berücksichtigt keine Entnahmemenge der Zielversion

(OSS note 812592 - MS64 ignore la quantité prélevée de la version cible)

entry.title: 2H 2024 SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding enhancement to the Internal Hire process.
entry.content:
Overview:

With 2H 2024, SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding has introduced additional enhancements to the Internal Hire process. These updates bring workflow support, cross-entity rule support, and position updates for internal hires initiated from SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting.

This blog explains the configuration options, validation mechanisms, and best practices for maximizing the benefits of these enhancements, ensuring a smoother and more efficient internal hiring experience.

Look back at the 2H 2023 release when we made the feature “Enable Enhanced Internal Hire Process” Automatically on. This feature leverages capabilities of Centralized Services in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central, offering data consistency between Recruiting and Employee Central.

Let’s roll back to the 2H 2024 release when we released the additional feature “Enable Additional Internal Hire Activities” to provide such as workflow support, cross-entity rule support, and position updates for internal hires initiated from SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting.

These switches are available in the Admin Center -> Onboarding General Settings page.

These switches are applicable only for internal hires initiated from the SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting flow.

Let us deep dive into these configuration options, validation mechanisms, and best practices for maximizing the benefits.

Let me refer to Switch 1 and Switch 2 as below.

Switch 1: Enable Enhanced Internal Hire Process

Switch 2: Enable Additional Internal Hire Activities

 

2H 2023

2H 2024

Configuration Switches

Switch 1 is made Automatically ON and you must keep this switch ON.

Note: This switch will be universal in 1H 2025.

You can refer blog for details https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blogs-by-sap/elevating-internal-transfers-transitions-through-sap-successfactors/ba-p/13579221

 

Switch 2 is added to support functionalities such as workflow support, cross-entity rule support, and position updates which were not available with Switch 1.

If you wish to use workflow support, cross-entity rule support, and position updates then you must switch on both (Switch 1 +  Switch 2)

Recruit to hire data mapping

RCM-mapped data is saved in EC  on successful validation. Existing EC data will be retained if Data is not mapped from RCM.

RCM-mapped data is saved in EC  on successful validation. Existing EC data will be retained if Data is not mapped from RCM.

Data Validations

Data validation and Data saving for HRIS entities are supported by Centralized Services in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central.

The process aligns with MSS UI save and validation ensuring all necessary validations are triggered before initiating cross-boarding.

 

Data validation and Data saving for HRIS entities are supported by Centralized Services in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central

The process aligns with the Recruiting - Employee Central (EC) internal hire flow, ensuring all necessary validations are triggered before initiating cross-boarding.

Functionalities

Workflow support, cross-entity rule support, and position updates for internal hires are not supported with Switch 1 “ ON”.

Workflow support, cross-entity rule support, and position updates for internal hires are supported with (Switch 1 + Switch 2) “ ON”.

Rule Context

Rule Context should be set up as “Onboarding” = Yes and the rest other as “No”.

 

Rule Context should be set up as “ESS/MSS = Yes” regardless of the other contexts

Note: The existing rule with “ESS/MSS = Yes” will get executed for Initiate Cross boarding.

If you want to execute rules for ESS/MSS UI’s but not for crossboarding then please review and change the rule conditions to avoid validation errors.

You can set rule context “ ESS/MSS = No” if rules are not expected to be executed for Crossboarding or ESS/MSS UI’s.

Frequently asked questions:

1. Why are validation errors shown on the initiate onboarding for internal hire after enabling switch 1 or switch 2?

Ans: With this enhancement, EC HRIS entity data save is done using Centralized services in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central which validates data consistency between Recruiting and Employee Central. To start the Internal Hire Process successfully, with the workflow and position follow-up updates, you need to ensure all required validations are met. This includes position-related checks and other EC Entities Onsave or Onchange rules execution.

2. What are possible ways to check and resolve position validation errors?

Ans: Please refer to the below example for error and possible resolution

  • Error: "[jobInfo]: You have selected a position that can only be assigned to one employee at a time."
    Solution: Verify the target position and adjust the start date or FTE (Full-Time Equivalent) accordingly.
  • Error: "Assigning this employee to the selected position would result in multiple incumbents on two occasions during the period from … until … Please choose another position."
    Solution: Check the target position and ensure it is available for the intended start date.

3. What are possible ways to check and resolve Pay Component Recurring Errors?

  • Error: Crossboarding failures due to incorrect pay component mappings.
  • Solution: Refer to the following Help guide and KBA articles to ensure correct pay component mapping.
    • Mapping Data from Recruiting Management with Employee Central Entities | SAP Help Portal
    • https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3204900

Conclusion:

The "Enable  Additional Internal Hire Activities" switch ON ensures alignment with the Recruiting-Employee Central (EC) internal hire flow, triggering all necessary validations before initiating the process.

Manages data persistence and data validation between Recruiting and Employee Central using  Centralized services in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central enhances validation accuracy.

Backend data validation in Onboarding is improved using Centralized Services functionalities in Employee Central.

entry.title: Re: Posting customer invoices in SAP S/4HANA 2023 TM
entry.content:
Hi Michael,

Great update. Is the same functionality we have developed and implemented in a large retail customer with S4HANA 2020.
We will analyse this new option after the upgrade project to S4HANA 2023 with go-live date in March ends.
Do you know if the Customer Invoice field is also available in UUII lists such as Item Hierarchy or Worklists?

And what about intercompany customer invoices? Are these part of the scope?
Thanks in advance.

Regards.

entry.title: Re: Compass - Simulation - Currency conversion not detect...
entry.content: Hi Eric, thanks for the screenshots! I see that you are using the currency conversion rate table - this is not supported as a driver. To enable your use case, you will need to remodel your currency conversion values as a leaf account member or a base measure, and also adjust your calculation for revenue accordingly to integrate this currency conversion rates into its calculation. Cheers, Chen Nee

entry.title: Re: Announcing the Water Track at the SAP for Energy and Utilities Conference 2025
entry.content:
Hello @MiquelCarbo 

is the presentation topic of "SAP Business Data Cloud" the same, we will be informed about at "SAP Business Unleashed" this Thursday evening (CET)?

It seems that everyone is making a huge secret of the topic, so could it be your blog is "whistleblowing"? 😅 ... in two days we'll know it.

BR, Martin

entry.title: SAP MM - What material features determine that I have to create a new material
entry.content:
SAP MM - What material features determine that I have to create a new material (enter a new ID number) and what changes can I make without changing the ID number?

I mean the data that I provide during the MM01 process

entry.title: Interview Assessment Form - Rating Scales - as dropdown list
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

1. The client wants the Rating Scales in the interview Assessment Form to appear as a dropdown list with below values

  • Very Good
  • Good
  • Average
  • Below Average

2. Also, the overall rating should be appearing as another picklist with values:

  • Strongly Recommended
  • Recommended
  • May be Recommended
  • Not Recommended

Attached is the screenshot, I have highlighted the expectation in the black rectangles.

Best Regards,

Sasi

entry.title: Re: Decentralized EWM on an SAP S/4HANA stack
entry.content:
Hello @MNaidenova 

You should definitely not activate EWM unless you are using it. The process of material documents posting with 30 mins delay and locking is not normal. That should be reported to SAP support.

You can also share the code that is blocking postings here along with EWM tables that are accessed during postings - I'll see if I can make any sense of it.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Subsequent Activation of non-leading ledger in S4 HANA
entry.content: Hello, did you figure this out? I think you will need SAP Migration service for Scenario 7, at least it was needed for ECC... not sure if it the same for S4H.

entry.title: Re: Compass - Simulation - Currency conversion not detect...
entry.content: Hi Chenn, okay I understand, thank you for clarifying this! Kind regards, Eric

entry.title: Jobs posted to a specific brand is being displayed on the Main Brand - Recruiting Marketing
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I have a client who recently implemented a new brand (Brand B) for their external site. However, they are facing an issue where jobs posted under Brand B are also appearing on the default brand. According to the KBA (2956361 - Jobs posted to a specific brand are being displayed on the Main Brand - Recruiting Marketing), this is the expected behavior in SAP SuccessFactors.

I would like to ask if anyone has found a workaround for this situation. Additionally, if anyone else is experiencing the same issue and is interested in exploring a different approach, please find the link to the improvement request to SAP here: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/340901/?section=sectionRelated

I would greatly appreciate any feedback or potential solutions.

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Reforma Tributária LC 214 - Nota Fiscal Writer J1B1N/...
entry.content: Oi pessoal, Analisando os detalhes da reforma e entendendo melhor, talvez faça sentido usar a condição IPI0 visto que o IPI não será extinto pelo governo por enquanto. Ele apenas será reduzido para alíquota zero. Talvez esse seja o fundamento da SAP para manter o hard code dos grupos de impostos tradicionais. Por enquanto não encontrei nada que explique o comportamento, essa é apenas uma análise minha e hipótese. Exemplo Obrigado, Welington Ottoni

entry.title: Maximum double value in report appears to be 1.7E306 instead of the expected 1.7E308. Why is that?
entry.content:
Crystal reports for visual studio, service pack 37

My report accesses a database with a column whose type is double.

The maximum double value according to IEEE format is 1.79769313486231E+308

However, the maximum value is can display in my report is 1.79769313486231E+306

Note I'm displaying it in default format: 1.80e+306

If the value is any larger, my display is 1.#Je+000

If I attempt to pass the larger value to UFL function whose input param is double, the value passed is infinity.

As a test and to eliminate my database and UFL from the suspect list, I created a formula that simply returns a number.  Since I could not figure out how to specify a value using exponential notation in the formula I used the exp function which looks like:

numbervar num;

num := Exp(705.1775427);

That value is approximately 1.79769313486231E+306.  If I try to increase the 705.1775427 param any higher, crystal gives me a 'numeric overflow' when saving the formula.

It appears that Crystal is artificially limiting my maximum value.  Is that true and why?

entry.title: Unable to change Target Ledger Group in Depreciation Area
entry.content: I am creating a new Depreciation Area with reference to an existing one. However, the Target Ledger Group in copied Dep area is not editable . Could any one suggest..

entry.title: LMS API - Get list of Authorized Instructors?
entry.content:
One of our business units has a tech team that's working on a solution using the LMS API to feed information into one of their systems. They are trying to get the list of which courses a particular instructor is authorized to teach. I know there's a report that we can generate, but they want to do everything through the API if possible.

I looked through the API documentation, but I was not able to find anything relevant. Has anyone else had success in trying to get this data using the API?

entry.title: Re: API Testing with Tricentis Test Automation integrated with SAP Cloud ALM & SAP Test Automati
entry.content:
Dear Andy, yes it is possible. 

You should decouple this call into 2 API calls:

  1. Capture the access_token from the authentication response and put it on a Buffer.
  2. Use {access_token} or {B[access_token]} in subsequent API calls (e.g., Authorization header).

entry.title: Re: ST-PI 740 v29 does not exist
entry.content:
Hello Aribeb,

ST-PI was available for download until yesterday. Looks like SAP pulled SP29 off the download area - probably because of errors. Since installation of the ST-PI SP29 we were facing errors in SM21 about database access.

Just try to proceed your SUM without SP29. You might have to redownload your Stack.xml without SP29 in it.

Regards
Daniel

entry.title: Re: Subsequent Activation of non-leading ledger in S4 HANA
entry.content:
Usually some SAP service is required. Look for :

  • OSS Recommendations - 1227504 - Subsequent implementation of an additional ledger
    NOTE: SAP General Ledger Migration service don't offer any service for S4H system. To get the current update on the available scenarios for S4H system, please raise message in component FIN-MIG-GL.
  • Support - Special Requirements for Subsequent Implementation of Further Functionality -  Scenario 7 -  subsequent implementation of a further ledger.

entry.title: Re: NWDS - NWBPM Debugging error
entry.content:
Hi,

I had exact problem and managed to fix it by clearing "Secure Storage":

After that during first debug session logon window was opened where I entered my credentials.

Thanks

entry.title: Difference between job type "Synch HRIS data" and "HRIS Sync"
entry.content:
We noticed that there are two different job types referring to HRIS Synchronization. Question is what is the difference between those? We noticed that one job type is being triggered by Provisioning and one from the instance.

Is there a different scope for those job types? If yes which are those?

 

Thanks in advance for your input.

entry.title: Re: SAP MM - What material features determine that I have...
entry.content: If the material is Serialised and Batch Managed and we want to remove it, we would have to create a new material. There is alternative way by creating a serialised profile without actually creating a serial number but I believe a better approach would be to create a new material.

entry.title: Re: From User Exits to RAP: Adopting a Clean Core Approach in S/4HANA
entry.content: Wonderful blog. @RavdeepSingh

entry.title: Re: Reforma Tributária LC 214 - Nota Fiscal Writer J1B1N/...
entry.content: Olá, IPI0 é para isento-não tributado, por ser aliq reduzida a zero, entendo que o fiscal irá optar por ICM2, pq se faturar devolução precisa informar mesmo que com base reduzida a zero.

entry.title: Account Information not recognized: Active Directory authentication failed to log you on. valid mapp
entry.content:
Hello,

We are using BO reports from coupe of yrs and recently observed that few users are not able to logon to system who were able to logon earlier. No changes in security and get error " Account Information not recognized: Active Directory authentication failed to log you on. valid mapping group FWM 00006 ". Users who have issue, when they logon from other machine that's on windows 11 can logon as expected. 

Observed that when we upgrade user device windows from 10 to 11, we are having this issue. Any suggestions on this issue.

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA - XSA Integration with Adobe Document Services
entry.content:
The ADS solution based on the JAVA Netweaver stack have an EOL to end of 2027 like other Business Suite products.

So SAP have the commitment of start developing the same functionality on XSA. Isn't a product yet...

entry.title: Re: How to use ABAP Test Cockpit (ATC) in the cloud as a developer in an on-premise system
entry.content:
Hi Kavita,

1. How many custom objects in the system you check during such an ATC run?
2. If you change a piece of code in a program for example: does the whole baseline get removed or just the findings related to this program appear again in the upcoming ATC run?

Kind Regards,
Olga.

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA - XSA Integration with Adobe Document Services
entry.content: That seems to be all I've found as well.

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite- Deep dive into Content Modifier
entry.content:
I created the same but getting this error:

org.apache.camel.language.bean.RuntimeBeanExpressionException: Failed to invoke method: replaceAll('X','') on null due to: org.apache.camel.component.bean.MethodNotFoundException: Method with name: replaceAll('X','') not found on bean: org.apache.camel.converter.stream.InputStreamCache@35f8df42 of type: org.apache.camel.converter.stream.InputStreamCache. Exchange[ID-244c92b0-2d65-459f-7bf6-55c5-1739106777462-492-1], cause: org.apache.camel.component.bean.MethodNotFoundException: Method with name: replaceAll('X','') not found on bean: org.apache.camel.converter.stream.InputStreamCache@35f8df42 of type: org.apache.camel.converter.stream.InputStreamCache. Exchange[ID-244c92b0-2d65-459f-7bf6-55c5-1739106777462-492-1]

entry.title: Re: Can we assign "Manage Pending Recruit" tasks to HR groups based on company code permis
Annual NZ Payroll User Group Meeting - Wellington, New Zealand
entry.content:
Don't miss out on the chance to network with peers in the payroll industry and keep up-to-date with the newest advancements in the field. This year's gathering is set to be both informative and captivating, offering essential updates and valuable networking experiences.

Agenda at a glance:
SAP Update – SAP New Zealand
Payroll Continuous Improvements – EPI-USE
Navigating Payroll Compliance – EPI-USE Labs

Speaker:
Katherine Truman, Localization Product Manager, SAP Globalization

For more information and details about the agenda, please visit here.

REGISTRATION LINK

If you want to get more information about the New Zealand Payroll topics provided by SAP experts, join the New Zealand Payroll - Human Capital Management Localization Work Zone group.

entry.title: Setting up the configuration of the ABAP Cloud in SAP Build
entry.content:
SAP has introduced new ABAP RAP capabilities in Build, generating interest among developers eager to explore these features using their free trial accounts. The following steps will help you to get started:

1. Login to the BTP Cockpit

Login to the BTP Cockpit to access your global account.

2. Subscribe to Build Code and ABAP

Navigate to the ‘Boosters’ tab and follow these steps:

  • Subscribe to 'Get started with SAP Build Code' and click ‘Start’ to install it.
  • Subscribe to 'Prepare an Account for ABAP Trial' and click ‘Start’ to install the ABAP development environment.

3. Create a New Instance of ABAP Environment

Go to ‘Account Explorer,’ then navigate to your sub-account. Next, follow these steps:

  • Go to the ‘Service Marketplace,’ click on 'ABAP Environment' to create a new instance.

  • In the left panel, navigate to ‘Instances & Subscriptions,’ click on the '1 Key' of the ABAP Environment, and download the file which you will use it later to configure the destination.

4. Configure the Destination

To configure the destination, follow these steps:

  • Go to the 'Connectivity' tab in the left panel, then navigate to 'Destinations.'
  • Click on ‘Import Destination’ and import this file.
  • Name your destination, and copy and paste the ‘URL,’ ‘Client ID,’ and ‘Client Secret’ from the '1 Key' file downloaded in step 3. Add your User (email) and Password.
  • For the ‘Token Service URL,’ copy the URL from the file you download in step 3, append /oauth/token at the end of the URL, and then save.
  • Check out this link for more details

5. Create an Application with ABAP Cloud

To create an application with ABAP Cloud:

  • Go to ‘Instances and Subscriptions,’ click on ‘SAP Build Code,’ then ‘Create’ and ‘Build an Application.’
  • Select 'ABAP Cloud.'

In the System Dropdown, you will see the list of Destinations connected to the BTP ABAP Environment. Select the destination pointing to your desired ABAP System. If the destination is valid, the System ID and System Client will display below the System's Dropdown.

a. Use an Existing Package

  • If you have a preexisting package, click 'Next' to move directly to the ABAP project creation dialog.

b. Create a New Project as follows:

6. Create a Package and Connect Eclipse to SAP Build

To create a package, we need to connect Eclipse to the web system. Follow these steps:

  • Download and install Eclipse.
  • Launch Eclipse, click on the ‘Help’ tab at the top, and select ‘Install New Software.’ Add this link: https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/latest, then select ‘ABAP development Tools’ and ‘Modeling tools for SAP BW/4HANA and SAP BW powered by SAP HANA.’
  • Click ‘Next’ >> ‘Next’, accept the terms of the license agreement, then click 'Finish.'

  • Select Authority/update site and click ‘Trust Selected.’ After completing the installation, click ‘Restart Now.’

  • Click on ‘File’ >> ‘New’ >> ‘Other’ >> Expand ‘ABAP’ and select ‘ABAP Cloud Project’ >> Click ‘Next.’

  • Copy the Destination URL from the BTP Cockpit and paste it into the ‘ABAP Service Instance URL,’ then click ‘Next.’

  • Click on ‘Copy Logon URL to Clipboard,’ paste it into your browser to log on successfully, then return to Eclipse and click ‘Finish.’

Return to the Lobby and click on the new ABAP Cloud project you created, then click 'Open eclipse.exe.'

In Eclipse:

  • Select ‘Open an existing project’ and choose your project.

Now, you can create an ABAP project or package from the Lobby

entry.title: Send a Commit Work with a Soap Call
entry.content:
Spoiler

Hello dear community,

I am facing a challenge when creating a sales order using BAPI_SALESORDER_CREATEFROMDAT2 via SOAP call with Postman.
The order creation works, but the BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT is not being sent, which means the order is not being finalized and saved.

Has anyone experienced this before and knows how to correctly send the BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT via SOAP call?
Do I need to combine both BAPIs in one request or send them separately?

Thank you for your help! 😊

entry.title: Re: Best practice recommendations on "Save all objects to...
entry.content: This issue happens a lot with offshore workers, especially those using VPN. The VPN adds its own lag, so you want to avoid VPN whenever you can. Additional tricks are to use an ethernet cable instead of WiFi to connect your PC to your network router, and pay for the fastest internet connection you can afford.

entry.title: Re: How to Start with Custom Situations: Design Your Situation Object (3/4)
entry.content: Hi @ravish_ranka, Situation Handling supports custom SOTs & SONTs with PCE 2408 that allow you to create custom objects. In former versions of Situation Handling you can build custom situation objects on SAP delivered objects.

entry.title: Re: Report download to Excel and special characters
entry.content: I think it's ANSII (sorry I'm such a technical neophyte) can a user change their character set somehow? Is this an excel thing? Maybe I can shift this to someone else to solve!! lol

entry.title: SAP SPM Incentive Management – Advanced Workflow White Paper – Web Actions
entry.content:
Introduction

This document explains Web Actions scripts. Workflow provides Web Action scripts that enable third parties to collaborate with Workflow by sending data in JSON or XML formats. Workflow can unpack the data received with the Web Action script.

This document illustrates how an external application can change the status of a case and close it.

Assumptions

This document assumes that you have prior knowledge and experience with the following SAP SPM applications:

  • IAS Administration
  • Advanced Workflow

You will need to work with your team to get Administration permissions to these 2 applications on your GCP Tenant.

Also, you need to know the mechanisms of how to call Workflow REST APIs and for this, you will need to use a REST client to execute the APIs.

The examples provided in this document use 'Bruno' as the client.

Description

Web Action is a script type which allows your third-party applications to collaborate with Workflow by sending data in JSON / XML formats. In the Web Action script, Workflow application can unpack the data received and then your script can execute some custom code.

A use case, for example, could be performing external background checks. In an onboarding workflow for new producers, you might want to accept requests only if the background check is successful. Once a new onboarding case is created in your workflow, the case could remain in a temporary status while a request is sent to an external application to initiate the background check. This process may take a few days. When the background check is completed, the third-party application can send the results to Workflow. These results can then be processed by a Workflow Web Action script, where you can implement the necessary logic to move the case to the next workflow status if the background check is positive, or to close and reject the case if the background check is negative.

This document shows another example, a simple example to receive a case key to close the case and move it to “Closed” status.

Pros / Cons

With Web Action scripts, you can interact with third-party applications from within our Workflow application and implement complex business logic directly in your scripts, keeping your business logic centralized in our Workflow Groovy scripts.

This capability is incredibly powerful, providing you with a great deal of flexibility.

It allows you to seamlessly integrate external systems, automate processes, and ensure consistency across your workflows.

Additionally, you can handle advanced scenarios such as validating external data, triggering actions in other systems, or dynamically updating workflow statuses based on external inputs, all while maintaining a single source of truth for your business logic.

Code

This section provides an example of how Web Actions work.

First, we will demonstrate a Workflow Groovy script of the Web Action type that receives a JSON package containing the case key of the case we want to close and move to “Closed” status.

Next, we will show how to send the JSON package and execute the Web Action script via API.

1. Create Web Action Script:

From Workflow, go to Setup > DEVELOPMENT > Scripts and create a new script of "Web Action" Type with name “Close Case Web Action” (system Id = close_case_web_action):

Next, copy and paste the following code to your script:

 

import groovy.json.JsonSlurper;

/*
This script receives a JSON from an external application with the case key that we want to close 

Example:

{ 
	“cases”:
	[ 
		{ 
			“case_key”:“RMA-A-123”
		}
	] 
}
*/

logger.debug("Test Web Action - START")

// external application defines format expected by Workflow 

response.setContentType("application/json")  

def slurper = new JsonSlurper() 
def returnStatus = ""

try {

  // gets JSON sent from an external application

  def text = request.getBody() 
  
  // Parse a text representation of a JSON data structure

  def json = slurper.parseText(text) 
  
  // Get case key we want to close
  
  def caseKey = json.cases[0].case_key
  
  logger.debug("CaseKey: " + caseKey)
  
  // get case key from parsed JSON
  
  def currentCase = resp.getCase(caseKey) 
  
  // case will end up in this status
  
  def status = resp.getStatus('closed') 

  // close case and move case to status closed  

  currentCase.setStatus(status) 
  currentCase.close()
  resp.update(currentCase)
  
  // Set OK response message
  
  returnStatus = """
  { 
  	"status"  : "OK",
	"message" : "Cases closed successfully"
  }"""

} catch(e) {

  logger.error(e)
  
  // Set Error response message
  
  returnStatus = """
  { 
  	"status"  : "FAILED",
	"message" : "${e.getMessage()}"
  }"""
  
}

// sends response message to the external application in JSON format

response.writer.write(groovy.json.JsonOutput.toJson(returnStatus))
response.writer.close()

 

As you can see, this code gets the case key in JSON format, the case is closed with Closed status, and it returns a success or error response also in JSON format.

To be able to execute this script, you need to call a Workflow API what is explained on the next section.

2. Call Web Action from an External Application:

For this example, we will use Bruno, a REST API client.

As with all Workflow APIs, the first step is to authenticate using the mechanism outlined in the Workflow REST API documentation.

NOTE: For a more detailed explanation about the authentication process, you can refer to the SAP Blog created by the Advanced Workflow Virtual Practice group, which provides an in-depth guide on authentication:

Call Workflow REST APIs in GCP 

As explained before, the Web Action script is expecting to receive the data in JSON format with the case key of the case that we want to close with Closed status.

The following JSON code is an example of what the external system should send for this example:

 

{ 
   “cases”:
   [ 
      { 
         “case_key” : “RMA-A-2”
      }
   ] 
}

 

To run this example, please verify your Workflow tenant and copy the case key of an existing case (i.e.: RAM-SAP-2):

 Authentication:

To run the Workflow API, you first need to execute the Authentication REST API to obtain the access token required for Workflow API authentication.

NOTE: To run the Workflow APIs, you must first complete the necessary configuration. Please visit Additional Resources section, where you will find a link to the How to Call Workflow REST APIs white paper.

Before you begin, ensure you have the following information available:

Field

Example

Workflow application internal host name

xg3-wf-sbox.workflow.cloud.sap

IAS host name

azr7m8zct.accounts.ondemand.com

Client ID

dddde129-de6e-47c1-95fc-646b38259a7a

Client Secret

XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX

Tenant

g0cb

You need to obtain a JWT access token using your Client ID and Client Secret and once you have the token, you can configure Bearer authentication to call the Workflow API.

To get the JWT access token, send a POST request to https://<sap_ias_hostname>/oauth2/token

You need to use Basic Authorization passing these 2 values:

Username

[Client ID]

Password

[Client Secret]

With these 2 Header parameters:

grant_type

client_credentials

Domain

[Tenant]

Bruno Sample:

Authorization:

Header Params:

Response:

Now that you have the JWT access token you can call the Workflow API.

3. Call Web Action:

The URL that calls the Web Action must be received in the following format:

https://[WORKFLOW_URL]/wpm/mt/[TENANT]/[DATA_FORMAT]/[SCRIPT_SYSTEM_ID]?

In this example, the external application sends the following URL:

https://xg3-wf-sbox.workflow.cloud.sap/wpm/mt/g0cb/json/close_case_web_action?

The URL contains the following example values:

Workflow URL

 xg3-wf-sbox.workflow.cloud.sap

Tenant

 g0cb

File Format

 json

Web Action Script System Id

 close_case_web_action

After obtaining the Access Token (as explained before), you can use it to call the Workflow REST API. Include the token in your API header using Bearer authentication.

When calling the Workflow API you need to use the following Header parameters:

Content-Type

application/json

domain

[Tenant]

Authorization

Bearer [Access Token]

Finally, you will need to pass the JSON data in your API body request.

Bruno Sample:

Header Params:

Body:

Response:

After the Web Action script is executed the case in Workflow is closed with Closed status as expected:

Conclusion

After reading this article you can call Web Services from external third-party application to interact with Advanced Workflow application.

Additional Resources

Advanced Workflow online documentation – Web Actions:

Web Actions Help

Bruno online documentation:

What is Bruno? – Bruno Docs

How to Call Workflow REST APIs:

Call Workflow REST APIs in GCP

entry.title: Re: Elevate Your SAC Dashboards: Unleash the Power of Custom R Visualizations!
entry.content: Very clear and crisp Soniya. Very helpful.

entry.title: Re: Writting data into table
entry.content: The link is not working anymore

entry.title: Re: Report download to Excel and special characters
entry.content: Consider Unicode UTF-8 or more, or at- least ISO-8859  (most commonly Windows Latin 1), as good old ANSI (Code page 437) doesn't handle most accented characters as "é" of "Québec" in your case it was unable to convert a 2 byte character.

entry.title: Re: Subsequent Activation of non-leading ledger in S4 HANA
entry.content: Thnks Raymond!

entry.title: Re: Inter-company dropship process
entry.content:
Not sure what the challenge you have here in this process. Once the USA plant goods issued, they can certainly generate either Intercompany Invoice or normal billing document on UK plant. Not sure, from legal perspective, how currently, it is configured.
Then, from UK plant, they can follow the normal process to other customers.
If at all, UK plant needs to add some extra material, it should be extended in that plant also without which, it is not possible. If they dont want to have inventory impact, you can control it via oms2

entry.title: Re: Subsequent Activation of non-leading ledger in S4 HANA
entry.content: I add this KBA (FIN-MIG-GL) 3167506 - Subsequent Implementation of a Further Accounting Principle for SAP S/4HANA.

entry.title: Re: From User Exits to RAP: Adopting a Clean Core Approach in S/4HANA
entry.content: Thank you @RavdeepSingh  & @Balaji_Guptha for the detailed explanation. This will be very helpful to many people.

entry.title: Re: SF - Time Sheet - Recording of Allowances

Hi Evangelia,

The business rule scenario was already set up as you suggested. There must be another reason why the rule is not triggered correctly. 

Anyway thank you for all your help and suggestions. Very much appreciated. 

Kind regards, 

Alex

entry.title: Re: SAPUI5 & OData: Multiple service calls vs single serv...
entry.content:
I would go b.

 

If the answer is b) then how do we structure the OData service and related manifest.json to retrieve all the required data from the one OData service?

don't know what you are talking

entry.title: Re: Facing Problems in screen navigation after adding a New custom tab to MM03
entry.content:
Problem - unwanted auto navigation in custom tabs especially when scrolling in table control. 

Solution - go to config table V_T133D (SM30) and populate checkbox field SLBKZ (Indicator: Process same screen again) for the record containing the "Local Screen" field value, "Successor Screen" value, and the function code in question.  Apparently SAP uses the record containing local screen, successor screen, and FCODE value to control auto-navigation.   

In my case scrolling in the table control produced a blank FCODE so after the checkbox is populated in V_T133D then a blank FCODE (scrolling) does not trigger a navigation to the successor screen.

entry.title: Re: Special Procurement Costing for Configurable Materials
entry.content:
Hi Billbartley,

Note 1121980 is still available in SAP and here is the link for that.

1121980 - Message CK393: stock transfer of config materials in costing - SAP for Me

entry.title: SuccessFactors Stories issue: Codes appearing in dimension input control
entry.content:
Hello--we are having the following issue in a dimension input control.  

This occurs randomly and will appear to our report consumers, which is a very bad user experience. Per SAP Support, the only way to fix it is to remove and re-add the fields to the dimension input control.  SAP support has told us that this is due to a metadata change and it is a known issue.  We don't understand how the metadata changes and are dissatisfied with how this is working.   

Has anyone else experienced this?  Have you come up with any workarounds?

Thanks      

@MissZP

entry.title: SAP EAM
entry.content:
In SAP PM module 

After TECO'ing the order if we try to do any changes in the order we must get a BS013 informative message stating that the order is in Teco'd status 

But in our case BS013 is already written  in the abap logic  but still error is not thrown after changing of dates even after Teco'ing .

Can you suggest me why SAP is not throwing error?

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Employee Central & Recruiting: configuring the New Hire process integration
entry.content:
Hello,

I am still new in Recruiting module. Could you share info if this is possible to add job requisition number from completed job requisition number into hiring process in EC? 

kind regards,
Bartosz Zapalski

entry.title: Re: How to add days to an existing date via advanced form...
entry.content:
I had another think about what it was I was trying to achieve here and came up with a better solution.

In the model I opened up the Close_Date dimension and put it into user managed.

The dimension table was set to DAY MTH QTR YEAR so in Excel I just created a blank sheet and added dates of 2000-01-01 to 2030-12-31

For column 2 I just did an =A2+84 which added 84 days to col 1 and copied the formula to the end of the data (2030-12-31)

In my user managed date dimension I created a new property called Gestation of type date and pasted the value of my column into here, making sure my first value was lined up with the existing 2000-01-01.

Save the dimension and I now have an 84 day offset column I can use for the necessary joining of an other model so I can show the data in this model thrown forward by 84 days...

entry.title: How to keep Email Address from Customer & Vendor to BP ?
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We are in the middle of a S/4 conversion (from ECC6 EHP5).
Our customers/vendors have been migrated to Business Partners.
Our IT partner for this conversion asked us, before the CVI, from which role we wanted to take information like phone numbers, email address, fax numbers. We choose customer ones.
We checked, during our non regression tests in S/4, for the BP that have customer & vendor roles, those information and confirmed that they are missing for vendor ones.
Is there someone who knows how to proceed to keep both information in order to avoid loosing information ?

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Team goals are not numbered on the goal plan report
entry.content:
Have enabled team goals on a goal plan and when we generate a goal plan report, team goals are not numbered. Has anybody observed this issue with team goals ? 

 

SAP SuccessFactors Performance & Goals

entry.title: Re: SAPUI5 & OData: Multiple service calls vs single serv...
entry.content: If we create only one OData service with multiple entity sets, will all the required data be retrieved when the OData service is called or is there a need to create associations & navigations between the non-related entity sets?

entry.title: Re: Handling Large Data With SAP Cloud Platform Integration OData V2 Adapter
entry.content:
Hi

I have a requirement where I need to set an initiation $skip token so the iteration can start at a certain index, for example if the flow failed before the end of the loop. how would that work?

thanks

entry.title: Legacy data transfer and Make company code settings - Asset Accounting specific - different FYV
entry.content:
Hi,

we have a client moving from ECC to S/4HANA Public cloud and we are trying to get fixed asset values to reconcile. The target system uses fiscal year variant K4 (Cal year + 4 special periods) and in ECC their fiscal year has been 01.08-31.07. Their aim is to extend their current fiscal year so that it will last from 31.08.2024-31.12.2025.

Asset transfer date is 31.04-2025 so for go-live in the middle of the year we aim to take in legacy asset values for the situation per 31.12 and then add the 2025 transactions. When doing like this, we face a reconciliation difference between ECC and S4 in between book and tax. The amount appears to be the 5 periods of depreciations in year 2024. 

What settings should be change to overcome this reconciliation difference? I was already thinking of app Make company code settings - Asset Accounting specific and there in Depreciation area settings change the last day of last closed fiscal year to 31.07.2024 but the system does not allow me to do that. 

Please let me know what other options we have?

-ME-

I was thinking that this setting could be

entry.title: Required fields for BP creation
entry.content:
Hi all,

Is there a way to prevent BP (global) creation in case some required information are not provided ?
For French companies, we want 3 fields to be required, 1 for European Companies (excluding French ones)

and none for the rest of the world.
Thanks in advance

entry.title: IDOC (PEXR2003) Segment/Field used to populate Item Text in FBL5N transaction
entry.content:
Hello,

The Inbound IDOC use IDOC_INPUT_REMADV_CTR function module to process the IDOC_DATA, that has PEXR2003 as its Basic Type. Just wanted to know which segment and field is used to populate Item Text (under Additional Data) in FBL5N transaction?

If no relevant segment & field is available within PEXR2003, should I go for BADI or Enhancement Implementation? If yes, where I have to implement it?

Request guidance. Thank you.

entry.title: Syntax error There must be a space or equivalent character (":", ",", ".") after "a literal"
entry.content:
I've got the same syntax error in S/4HANA Public ABAP Cloud, as the one I had 4 years ago with ABAP 7.52, posted here: Solved: Syntax error There must be a space or equivalent c... - SAP Community. I guess I would have the same syntax error with BTP ABAP environment.

So, I have this syntax error:

There must be a space or equivalent character (":", ",", ".") after "`any text   `".

in this short class:

Source code:

 

CLASS zcl_bug_2_line_stmnt_quote_72 DEFINITION PUBLIC FINAL CREATE PUBLIC .
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS test.
ENDCLASS.
CLASS zcl_bug_2_line_stmnt_quote_72 IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD test.
    DATA(text) = `any text                                             `
&& `any text`.
  ENDMETHOD.  
ENDCLASS.

 

REASON: on the line 7, the ending back quote is at the position 72 followed by nothing, and the next line 8 starts at the position 1. The exact same issue happens if single quotes are used instead of back quotes.

SOLUTION: for instance, add a double quote after the position 72.

For information, in case French people have the same issue and are looking for a solution (and can find this thread), the message is:

"`any text   `" doit être suivi par un espace ou un caractère équivalent (":", ",", ".").

entry.title: Re: Syntax error There must be a space or equivalent char...
entry.content:
REASON:

The "bug" happens when there's an ending back quote or single quote at the position 72 followed by nothing, and the next line starts at the position 1.

SOLUTION:

Start the second line at least at the position 2.

For instance:

CLASS zcl_bug_2_line_stmnt_quote_72 DEFINITION PUBLIC FINAL CREATE PUBLIC .
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS test.
ENDCLASS.
CLASS zcl_bug_2_line_stmnt_quote_72 IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD test.
    DATA(text) = `any text                                             `
              && `any text`.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

entry.title: Question on Supplier Profile Summary on SLP (NGSM-2959)
entry.content:
Hi the (NGSM-2959) feature can only be installed on client realms (not on partner realms) which makes it impossible to test it on our partner realm.

Is it possible to display several operational scores (eg one logistic score and one quality score) or can there be only one ? 

Thanks in advance for your answer

Regards

Isabelle

SAP Supplier Lifecycle Management

entry.title: Change column (Generic property of a dimension) header in Analytic Application
entry.content:
Hello - I have a table visual in an Analytic Application. All the columns in the table are shown from the property of dimension except for the measure. By default, Name of the generic property is shown in the visual. I could not find an option to change it (tried to change it by double clicking on column header. But it did not work). Tried creating a calculated dimension. But in my case, it would not work as I want to show unbooked data from the dimension and it would show duplicate records based on the data in underlying model. 

 

Thanks,

Phani

There is a new understanding by the AT, published in Ofício Circulado n.º 20274, how the retention should be calculated for IRS Jovens 2025.

Do you have any estimation when SAP will return a solution for this?

Kind regards

Aurora Costa

entry.title: Re: SAP RISE - Key Learnings
entry.content:
@MustafaBensan I agree, I read the first sentence and it is wrong 😀

first comment is also very likely written with GenAI, it's just sad here 🫤

entry.title: Need a static Scaling Factor column added either to a Char/KF structure in BEx Query Designer
entry.content:
In BEx QD, is there a way to either add a free text field into the characteristic structure or maybe add a key figure that contains partial text? Our customer wants to have the scaling factor labels spelled out next to the goal amounts. As show below and attached. If not spelled out, they might settle for M/B next to the numbers. The scaling factor feature (at least in this version of the QD) seems to want to put it in the KF header as 1000000s or 1000000000s. We are on the 7.4 release of QD Revision 739. Any information about why this can or cannot be accomplished would be very helpful. Also, if you know it is possible in later versions of BW that might help as well. Also, we would like to do this without modifying the info providers if possible.

Thank you!

Chris

 

 1st Choice Solution*2nd Choice Solution**   
Area****GoalGoalGoals (hidden col.)Actuals% Actuals to Goal
      
Discretionary Support$123 Million$123M                    123,000,000$12,354,60010%
Future Needs  $321 Million$321M                    321,000,000$32,154,60010%
Operational Support     $1 Billion     $1B               1,000,000,000$225,460,00023%
Research     $2 Billion     $2B               2,000,000,000$825,460,00041%
Building            $200 Million$200 M                    200,000,000$20,254,60010%
Total                                    $3.64 Billion$3.64B               3,644,000,000$1,115,683,80031%
      
*      We would like to have different scaling factors possible for rows with the "Millions" and "Billions" labels spelled out. This could either be the separate scaling of a key figure column or, if possible, the insertion of a text field that cooresponds with the row characteristic nodes.
**     Same as the 1st choice but have "M" and "B" as the scaling indicators instead of being spelled out.
***   Goals (In case the goal is a text column this would be used for the % calculation but would be hidden)
**** This is a characteristic structure comprised of multiple characteristic selections along with formulas.

entry.title: Re: EWM Layout-Oriented Storage Control (LOSC) – A simple guide
entry.content: Thanks for sharing @Yuri_Nogueira, well written and very useful content!

entry.title: Extensibility of WM Handling Unit OPD or Add Resource Group to WM Handling Unit in OPD
entry.content:
To support efficient picking and packing in a distribution warehouse, we must allow users to be able to easily swap which mobile printer / print queue they are connected to. This must be allowed to be changed on an RF device to allow a user to swap on the floor, without having to access Output Parameter Determination, which is a Super-User level application.

We can achieve this by 1 of 2 ways.

  1. Adding Resource Group as a condition within WM Handling Unit in Output Parameter Determination. Currently, this business object is not extensible.
  2. Allowing this object, of 'WM Handling Unit' to be extensible allows us to add a custom field or resource group

 

By allowing determination of Printer Queue to Resource Group, a user can perform 05 Resource Management > 02 Change Resource Group on the standard RF device and enter their new resource group. This resource group can be linked to a print queue and thus to a specific printer. This allows a user to easily swap from one printer to another by utilizing this standard field. In SAP Private cloud, there is separate option available to allow a user to swap printers directly within the same RF screen as previously mentioned of 05 Resource Management > 03 Change Printer


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Extensibility of WM Handling Unit OPD or Add Resource...
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

Indeed the application object WM Handling Unit is not extensible, but you can use the application Object Handling Unit. This one is extensible.

There is a BAdI where custom fields can be filled. For details, see Extensibility for Adobe Output Forms of HU Documents | SAP Help Portal

Thank you and best regards,

Geovana

entry.title: Re: EWM Layout-Oriented Storage Control (LOSC) – A simple guide
entry.content: Great content @Yuri_Nogueira. Very detailed!

entry.title: Re: Publish the terminated employees in worksheet to EC
entry.content:
Hi Vivek,

It sounds like maybe you don't have access to search for inactive employees.

Try this permission -

CAUTION - this permission will open up searching for inactives across the system (People Profile, Talent module searches) and it does not respect RBP targets in all cases - so this permission should be given out with caution. If this is for an Admin in your case who already have access to everyone it should be fine but it should only be given out to other users when there is no other option as mentioned since it does not respect targets in all cases and could allow a user to search someone who is inactive who is outside their target.

Thanks,

Lesley

Corrected.

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Queue Contents Editing for Failed Queues from EWM to ERP
entry.content: Hi, Thank you Very much for sharing the information, I have made all the setting mentioned in this blog, but I couldn't able to see the change icon (pencil icon) in SMQ2 que container. Am I missing anything? Please suggest.

entry.title: Successfactors - report Integration center API payload not sent to iflow CPI
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

Could you help me on this very strange behavior of a successFactors API call ?

I created a report in successFractors to export JobInfo data to an API exposed in CPI.
I created a filter to send only one user's folder with the RUN NOW option.
In preview Data I see the user's data, in the Manager Execution log I exported the Page_1_dummy.json file which contains the data that should be sent in the body of the CPI API.
The problem in CPI, only the header of the Iflow contains data and there is no data in the payload, it is empty. No error in the execution of the Iflow CPI.
When I retrieve the data file from the Successfactors report and integrate it into a body in Postman. the data is sent to the Iflow in SCPI.
I execute my query in successfactors with an Admin user who has the necessary permissions. Have you already encountered this type of incident? Thank you for your feedback and advice. Best regards

entry.title: Re: Find materials with the status of "with replacement" but none set up in VB11

entry.title: Re: What is the maintenance order header data source (Tab...

Hello @hamza07 

 

The CDS view I_MaintenanceOrderDEX is available in S/4HANA Cloud for Header information

 

Yes , behind the CDS view is AUFK 

 

Best Regards

Enda

entry.title: Can anyone explain how to maintain flexible replenishment lead time in S4HANA aATP with BPS?
entry.content:
Dear Gurus,

Recently SAP has included the functionality to use API for Scheduling Consumers in order to retrieve flexible replenishment lead times using a Scheduling Schema for aATP.

I have read the documentation provided by SAP but honestly I feel a bit lost since it seems too technical. I thought it would be similar to the approach followed to Scheduling in BPS by maintaining durations and calendars in Fiori. However, it seems a bit more complex and I would like to know if someone can easily explain what and how it works.

Thanks a lot!!

entry.title: Re: Variable Pay 5 Configuration Files - EC Integrated
entry.content: Is there a question to this? Was this post meant for another forum?

entry.title: Change in Fiscal Year Variant
entry.content:
Dear All,

We set up our fiscal financial calendar to be January through December, however, we have realized that this should have been May through April to align with HMRC tax reporting.  We have some transactions in the system but have not closed any periods yet.  Can you help with putting in the new fiscal calendar, and advise on how to accomplish this / what are the steps to take?  


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Change in Fiscal Year Variant
entry.content:
Dear All,

It is not possible to switch to a different fiscal year variant for a set of books if journal entries exist for that set of books.

Refer to the below KBAs for additional information, please:

KBA 1825057 - How to Change Fiscal Year Variant https://me.sap.com/notes/1825057

KBA 2013755 - Fiscal Year Variant Cannot be Changed https://me.sap.com/notes/2013755

Regards,
Anderson
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Portuguese National Budget for 2025- Payroll Legal Changes

Hi Aurora,

Thanks and Regards,

Anabela Lúcio

entry.title: Re: Attachments in Project limit
entry.content:
Hi @Angelina_Mastro 

 

Files only up to a size of 100 MB can be uploaded using Attachment Service which covers the project, enterprise projects apps 

 

Best Regards

Enda

entry.title: MDK - pageProxy getActionBar not working on Android
entry.content:
I'm trying to control my Action Bar Item visibility through a rule, but am struggling on an Android device.  I've reproduced the issue in a simple test scenario below.

My test page has a Form Cell Simple Property.  In the OnValueChange event, I'm calling a rule.  In the rule, the issue that I'm running into is that on an Android device (Zebra TC51), calling clientAPI.getPageProxy().getActionBar() is failing.  It works on the webclient.

The OnValueChange event code is:

 

export default function TestChangeValueEventAndActionBar(clientAPI) {
    const bar = clientAPI.getPageProxy().getActionBar();
    if (typeof bar !== 'undefined') {
        alert ('bar good')
    }   
}

 

On the web client, I get the alert.  On the android device, I do not.

The other thing I've tried is accessing the properties directly:

 

    const bar2 = clientAPI.getPageProxy()._page._actionBar;
    if (typeof bar2 !== 'undefined') {
        alert ('bar2 good')
    } 
    const items = bar2._items;
    if (typeof items !== 'undefined') {
        alert ('items good')
    }

 

In this case, on Andriod we get "bar2 good" but not the second alert.  On web, we get both.

My end goal is that I want to show/hide or enable/disable the Action Bar Item when a value is entered in the Simple Property.  The strategy is that I have a rule in the Visible property of the Action Bar Item that returns true or false, and then on the value change event I call the redraw() method.  But I can't do that if the getActionBar doesn't give me the action bar.

Any advice?

entry.title: Re: Do OData Create & Delete Operation in UI5 Application on BAS for CAPM Full Stack UI5 Development
entry.content:
SAP HANA Cloud #CAPM SAP Fiori 

 #How can i crud operations with SAP HANA DB in SAP CAPM Project OData service how can create API

entry.title: Re: Analytic Model was created with older version of the...
entry.content:
I've also just ran into the exact same issue after the latest SAP Datasphere update. It’s super frustrating that suddenly the model is flagged as being created with an older version of the editor, and the only suggested fix is to delete and recreate everything. We also discovered that special characters (like “-”) in technical names are causing trouble these same fields worked fine before.

I also tried restoring from an older JSON export and redeploying the model, it partially worked in my case. 
I hope this helps and if anyone else has found a better workaround, I’d love to hear it!

entry.title: Re: Duplicate plan order orders for same sales order line...
entry.content:
Hello

It is very difficult to find out the root cause of the issue, without having enough information about it. 

What I can tell you is that the most frequent cause for duplicated orders generated by MRP is some problem in the rescheduling check. 

Sometimes MRP generates a planned order to cover a requirement and then you move it to the future when converting to production order. If the production order is outside the rescheduling horizon, or not included in the rescheduling check, then MRP might create a new planned order to cover the requirement. 

Best regards,
Caetano

entry.title: Re: SAPUI5 & OData: Multiple service calls vs single serv...
entry.content: I also go with b, if only because of batch. But I also don't understand what exactly you want to ask. It all depends on your data model and your app ... the best thing would probably be to have a look at the documentation to understand how it all works together.
